# Sproutflow Studio

Sproutflow Studio is a boutique digital studio in New Orleans, Louisiana that builds custom websites and the custom business software behind them for small businesses. Positioning: enterprise strategy, small business pricing. Websites get clients in the door; the systems behind them keep clients and save owners hours every week.

## Services

### Layer 1: Custom Website Design and Development
- Custom-designed, custom-built websites (Next.js, TypeScript)
- Conversion-focused lead capture, local SEO foundations, professional copywriting
- E-commerce storefront design (Shopify customization)

### Layer 2: Custom Business Software and Automation
This is what very few local providers offer, and what extends projects into the upper price tiers:
- Internal admin tools and dashboards (role-based tooling for owners, managers, staff)
- Custom CRM and customer management systems
- Lightweight CMS builds so owners update their own content
- Workflow automation: intake, follow-up, scheduling, document processing, missed-call response

## Service Area
- New Orleans metro area and South Louisiana (primary)
- Remote nationwide (most projects run fully remote)

## Pricing
- Projects range from $850 to $7,500+
- Starter and standard custom websites occupy the lower and middle range
- Custom systems work (CRMs, admin tools, automation) occupies the upper tiers
- Every project gets a fixed quote before work starts
- Website care plans (hosting, security, updates) from $200/month

## Client Results
- Second Line Psychiatry (New Orleans telehealth practice): 50% more qualified inquiries after launch, with booking connected to the practice's Google Business profile
- NOLA Pool Solutions (New Orleans metro): 30% customer acquisition growth and a #1 local Google ranking, starting from no website
- DJN Services LLC (North Alabama welding and hauling, veteran-owned): more bookings through a new quote-form site, delivered in 2 weeks
- Nealy Event Decor (Dallas luxury event fabrication): consultation requests doubled after launching an editorial brand site with a self-managed inventory CMS
